• Show All Code
  • Hide All Code

Cleaning

# Load packages
library(tidyverse)
## ── Attaching core tidyverse packages ──────────────────────── tidyverse 2.0.0 ──
## ✔ dplyr     1.1.4     ✔ readr     2.1.5
## ✔ forcats   1.0.1     ✔ stringr   1.6.0
## ✔ ggplot2   4.0.0     ✔ tibble    3.3.0
## ✔ lubridate 1.9.4     ✔ tidyr     1.3.1
## ✔ purrr     1.1.0     
## ── Conflicts ────────────────────────────────────────── tidyverse_conflicts() ──
## ✖ dplyr::filter() masks stats::filter()
## ✖ dplyr::lag()    masks stats::lag()
## ℹ Use the conflicted package (<http://conflicted.r-lib.org/>) to force all conflicts to become errors
library(ggplot2) 
library(readxl) 
library(lubridate)
library(broom)
library(viridis)
## Loading required package: viridisLite
# Global options
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

#importing functions
source("functions.r")

1 Introduction

This script contains the code used to clean the raw datasets accessed from CDC Wonder contained within the /RawData directory. All datasets generated from this script will be saved in the /CleanData directory.

Functions used to clean the datasets may be accessed from the /Scripts/functions.R file.

2 Female Monthly Assault Counts

rawdata_female <- read_excel("../RawData/MonthlyAssaults.xlsx") 
monthly_f <- rawdata_female %>% 
  mutate(month_year = my(Month)) %>% 
   select(-Notes,-Population,-"Crude Rate", -Month, -"Month Code") 
head(monthly_f) 
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   Deaths month_year
##    <dbl> <date>    
## 1     16 2018-01-01
## 2     16 2018-02-01
## 3     17 2018-03-01
## 4     26 2018-04-01
## 5     17 2018-05-01
## 6     11 2018-06-01
#excluding NA/provisional data: No data starting on Aug 2025. Total provisional data starts at Jan 2024. 

monthly_f <- monthly_f %>% 
  filter(month_year < "2024-01-01")

#creating a binary indicator variable to say whether the bill was in effect 
 
monthly_f <- monthly_f %>% 
  mutate(bill8 = month_year > "2021-08-01") #all months after August

monthly_f %>% 
  slice(35:55)
## # A tibble: 21 × 3
##    Deaths month_year bill8
##     <dbl> <date>     <lgl>
##  1     19 2021-01-01 FALSE
##  2     20 2021-02-01 FALSE
##  3     25 2021-03-01 FALSE
##  4     24 2021-04-01 FALSE
##  5     23 2021-05-01 FALSE
##  6     22 2021-06-01 FALSE
##  7     25 2021-07-01 FALSE
##  8     20 2021-08-01 FALSE
##  9     35 2021-09-01 TRUE 
## 10     24 2021-10-01 TRUE 
## # ℹ 11 more rows

We need to apply the same cleaning methodology, this time for Texas resident males in the same age group (15-44).

3 Male Monthly Assault Counts

rawdata_male <- read_excel("../RawData/MaleMonthlyAssaults.xlsx") 
monthly_m <- rawdata_male %>% 
  mutate(month_year = my(Month)) %>% 
   select(-Notes,-Population,-"Crude Rate", -Month, -"Month Code") 
head(monthly_m) 
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   Deaths month_year
##    <dbl> <date>    
## 1     89 2018-01-01
## 2     53 2018-02-01
## 3     90 2018-03-01
## 4     71 2018-04-01
## 5     63 2018-05-01
## 6     82 2018-06-01
#excluding NA/provisional data: No data starting on Aug 2025. Total provisional data starts at Jan 2024. 

monthly_m <- monthly_m %>% 
  filter(month_year < "2024-01-01")

#creating a binary indicator variable to say whether the bill was in effect 
 
monthly_m <- monthly_m %>% 
  mutate(bill8 = month_year > "2021-08-01") #all months after August

monthly_m %>% 
  slice(35:55)
## # A tibble: 21 × 3
##    Deaths month_year bill8
##     <dbl> <date>     <lgl>
##  1    136 2020-11-01 FALSE
##  2    137 2020-12-01 FALSE
##  3    127 2021-01-01 FALSE
##  4     99 2021-02-01 FALSE
##  5    104 2021-03-01 FALSE
##  6    124 2021-04-01 FALSE
##  7    137 2021-05-01 FALSE
##  8    131 2021-06-01 FALSE
##  9    119 2021-07-01 FALSE
## 10    119 2021-08-01 FALSE
## # ℹ 11 more rows

It should be noted we are missing death data for females for May 2019 (2019-05-01) and September 2019 (2019-09-01). We need to fill in this hole, so we will investigate filling this gap in the data by querying both genders and subtracting the deaths for males to reveal how many were among females.

rawdata_both_genders <- read_excel("../RawData/2019_monthly_both_sexes.xlsx") 
both_genders_2019 <- rawdata_both_genders %>% 
  mutate(month_year = my(Month)) %>% 
   select(-Notes,-Population,-"Crude Rate", -Month, -"Month Code") 
head(both_genders_2019)  
## # A tibble: 6 × 2
##   Deaths month_year
##    <dbl> <date>    
## 1     83 2019-01-01
## 2     98 2019-02-01
## 3    105 2019-03-01
## 4     81 2019-04-01
## 5    107 2019-05-01
## 6     93 2019-06-01
#selecting the dates we need 

both_genders_2019 %>% 
  filter(month_year %in% c("2019-05-01", "2019-09-01")) 
## # A tibble: 2 × 2
##   Deaths month_year
##    <dbl> <date>    
## 1    107 2019-05-01
## 2     96 2019-09-01
#107 in may, 96 in sept 

monthly_m %>% 
  filter(month_year %in% c("2019-05-01", "2019-09-01"))  
## # A tibble: 2 × 3
##   Deaths month_year bill8
##    <dbl> <date>     <lgl>
## 1     98 2019-05-01 FALSE
## 2     89 2019-09-01 FALSE
#98 in may, 89 in sept 

#may: 107-98 = 9
#sept: 96-89 = 7

#dropping the problematic rows for females
monthly_f <- monthly_f %>%
  filter(
    !(month_year %in% as.Date(c("2019-05-01", "2019-09-01")))
  )

monthly_f <- monthly_f %>%
  bind_rows(
    tibble(
      month_year = as.Date(c("2019-05-01", "2019-09-01")),
      Deaths     = c(11, 7),
      sex        = "Female",
      bill8      = FALSE
    )
  )

Next we are going to merge the datasets to create an all sex version.

monthly_all <- bind_rows( #combining datasets
  monthly_f %>% mutate(sex = "Female"),
  monthly_m %>% mutate(sex = "Male")
)

monthly_m %>% #checking which months are missing in the female df
  anti_join(monthly_f, by = "month_year") %>%
  select(month_year)
## # A tibble: 0 × 1
## # ℹ 1 variable: month_year <date>
monthly_all <- monthly_all %>%
  group_by(sex) %>% 
  arrange(month_year) %>%
  mutate(time = row_number())
range(monthly_all$time)
## [1]  1 72
monthly_f <- monthly_f %>%
  arrange(month_year) %>%
  mutate(time = row_number())
range(monthly_f$time)
## [1]  1 72
monthly_m <- monthly_m %>%
  arrange(month_year) %>%
  mutate(time = row_number())

range(monthly_m$time)
## [1]  1 72
#looks correct! Saving the datasets

write_csv(monthly_all, "../CleanData/monthly_all.csv")
write_csv(monthly_m, "../CleanData/monthly_m.csv")
write_csv(monthly_f, "../CleanData/monthly_f.csv")

4 All Cause Female Reproductive age

Next we are going to clean this dataset so we may examine how all-cause deaths may differ from assault only. This will be important as deaths by assault are underreported and often misclassified. Let’s start by importing the data and applying the cleaning function for .csv’s.

all_repro_f <- read.csv("../RawData/texas_allcause_female1544.csv")

all_repro_f <- clean.csv(all_repro_f)

head(all_repro_f) 
##   Deaths month_year bill8 time
## 1    436 2018-01-01 FALSE    1
## 2    378 2018-02-01 FALSE    2
## 3    354 2018-03-01 FALSE    3
## 4    336 2018-04-01 FALSE    4
## 5    380 2018-05-01 FALSE    5
## 6    355 2018-06-01 FALSE    6

Let’s check out the data:

ggplot(all_repro_f, aes(month_year, Deaths))+ 
  geom_line()

Looks good! Let’s save the all cause female data to our cleaned folder.

write_csv(all_repro_f, "../CleanData/all_cause_repro_f.csv")

Males this time:

all_repro_m <- read.csv("../RawData/texas_allcause_male1544.csv") 
glimpse(all_repro_m)
## Rows: 142
## Columns: 8
## $ Notes                                    <chr> "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "…
## $ Month                                    <chr> "Jan., 2018", "Feb., 2018", "…
## $ Month.Code                               <chr> "2018/01", "2018/02", "2018/0…
## $ Deaths                                   <int> 899, 726, 759, 741, 810, 835,…
## $ Population                               <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
## $ Crude.Rate                               <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
## $ Crude.Rate.Lower.95..Confidence.Interval <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
## $ Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
all_repro_m <- all_repro_m %>% 
  mutate(month_year = my(Month)) %>% 
  select(-Month, -Month.Code, -Notes,-Population:-Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval) %>% 
  filter(month_year < "2024-01-01") %>% 
  mutate( time = row_number ())


ggplot(all_repro_m, aes(month_year, Deaths))+ 
  geom_line() 

write_csv(all_repro_m, "../CleanData/all_cause_repro_m.csv")

5 Assault all age female

Next we are going to clean the data for all ages female, cod by assault.

all_age_f_assault <- read_excel("../RawData/All_Ages_Female.xlsx")
all_age_f_assault <- clean.xcel(all_age_f_assault) 
head(all_age_f_assault) 
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   Deaths month_year bill8  time
##    <dbl> <date>     <lgl> <int>
## 1     29 2018-01-01 FALSE     1
## 2     26 2018-02-01 FALSE     2
## 3     30 2018-03-01 FALSE     3
## 4     31 2018-04-01 FALSE     4
## 5     33 2018-05-01 FALSE     5
## 6     23 2018-06-01 FALSE     6
all_age_m_assault <- read.csv("../RawData/all_age_assault_male.csv")
all_age_m_assault <- all_age_m_assault %>% 
   mutate(month_year = my(Month)) %>% 
  select(-Month, -Month.Code, -Notes,-Population:-Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval) %>% 
  filter(month_year < "2024-01-01") %>% 
  mutate( time = row_number ()) 

write_csv(all_age_m_assault, "../CleanData/all_age_m_assault.csv")

Same as before, let’s briefly check out what the data looks like:

ggplot(all_age_f_assault, aes(month_year, Deaths))+ 
  geom_line()

Let’s write our csv:

write_csv(all_age_f_assault, "../CleanData/all_age_f_assault.csv")

6 Age 15+

Let’s also check out the data for texas deaths by assault, this time just with ages 15+.

assault_15_plus <- read_excel("../RawData/assault_15_plus.xlsx") 
assault_15_plus <- clean.xcel(assault_15_plus) 
head(assault_15_plus) 
## # A tibble: 6 × 4
##   Deaths month_year bill8  time
##    <dbl> <date>     <lgl> <int>
## 1     16 2018-01-01 FALSE     1
## 2     16 2018-02-01 FALSE     2
## 3     17 2018-03-01 FALSE     3
## 4     26 2018-04-01 FALSE     4
## 5     17 2018-05-01 FALSE     5
## 6     11 2018-06-01 FALSE     6
ggplot(assault_15_plus, aes(month_year, Deaths))+ 
  geom_line() 

write_csv(assault_15_plus, "../CleanData/assault_15_plus")

7 National control set

This time we are going to pull ages 15-44 deaths by assault nationally.

nat_f_assault <- read_excel("../RawData/national_f_assault.xlsx") 
nat_f_assault <- clean.xcel(nat_f_assault) 
head(nat_f_assault) 
## # A tibble: 6 × 6
##   Deaths Crude Rate Lower 95% Co…¹ Crude Rate Upper 95%…² month_year bill8  time
##    <dbl> <chr>                     <chr>                  <date>     <lgl> <int>
## 1    194 Not Applicable            Not Applicable         2018-01-01 FALSE     1
## 2    149 Not Applicable            Not Applicable         2018-02-01 FALSE     2
## 3    170 Not Applicable            Not Applicable         2018-03-01 FALSE     3
## 4    183 Not Applicable            Not Applicable         2018-04-01 FALSE     4
## 5    165 Not Applicable            Not Applicable         2018-05-01 FALSE     5
## 6    182 Not Applicable            Not Applicable         2018-06-01 FALSE     6
## # ℹ abbreviated names: ¹​`Crude Rate Lower 95% Confidence Interval`,
## #   ²​`Crude Rate Upper 95% Confidence Interval`
ggplot(nat_f_assault, aes(month_year, Deaths))+ 
  geom_line() 

write_csv(nat_f_assault, "../CleanData/nat_f_assault.csv")

8 Disaggregated Race

We pulled the deaths for assault and all-cause, but we are just going to focus on all-cause to increase power.

h <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/All_cause/H.csv")
nh <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/All_cause/NH.csv") 


racial <- clean_racial(nh,h) 
write_csv(racial, "../CleanData/racial_all_cause.csv")

9 COVID Deaths

We need to clean the data with deaths attributed to COVID so we may create an all-cause minus COVID mortality dataset.

Starting with pulling up the COVID mortality data (female reproductive age):

covid <- read.csv("../RawData/COVID/COVID.csv")
#checking import
glimpse(covid)
## Rows: 144
## Columns: 8
## $ Notes                                    <chr> "", "", "", "", "", "", "", "…
## $ Month                                    <chr> "Jan., 2018", "Feb., 2018", "…
## $ Month.Code                               <chr> "2018/01", "2018/02", "2018/0…
## $ Deaths                                   <chr> "0", "0", "0", "0", "0", "0",…
## $ Population                               <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
## $ Crude.Rate                               <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
## $ Crude.Rate.Lower.95..Confidence.Interval <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…
## $ Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval <chr> "Not Applicable", "Not Applic…

We have quite a few days where the death value is suppressed.

covid %>% 
  filter(Deaths == "Suppressed") %>% 
  select(Month)
##                                   Month
## 1                            Mar., 2020
## 2                            Apr., 2022
## 3                            Jun., 2022
## 4                            Sep., 2022
## 5                            Oct., 2022
## 6                            Nov., 2022
## 7                            Mar., 2023
## 8                            Apr., 2023
## 9                             May, 2023
## 10                           Jun., 2023
## 11                           Jul., 2023
## 12                           Aug., 2023
## 13                           Sep., 2023
## 14                           Oct., 2023
## 15                           Nov., 2023
## 16                           Dec., 2023
## 17                           Jan., 2024
## 18                           Feb., 2024
## 19                           Mar., 2024
## 20                           Apr., 2024
## 21                           Jun., 2024
## 22                           Jul., 2024
## 23                           Aug., 2024
## 24                           Sep., 2024
## 25                           Oct., 2024
## 26                           Nov., 2024
## 27                           Dec., 2024
## 28             Feb., 2025 (provisional)
## 29             Mar., 2025 (provisional)
## 30             Apr., 2025 (provisional)
## 31             May., 2025 (provisional)
## 32             Jul., 2025 (provisional)
## 33             Aug., 2025 (provisional)
## 34             Sep., 2025 (provisional)
## 35 Feb., 2026 (provisional and partial)
#Need to change suppressed values to be 0s and make deaths numeric. 
covid <- covid %>% 
    mutate(Deaths = as.numeric(na_if(Deaths, "Suppressed")),
          month_year = my(Month)) %>% 
  select(-Notes,-Month, -"Month.Code", -Population:-"Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval") %>% 
  filter(month_year < "2024-01-01") %>% 
  mutate(time = row_number ())

Next, we will create new COD variables so when we delete the deaths from all cause we can match up accordingly:

covid <- covid %>% 
  mutate(cod = "covid")

all_repro_f <- all_repro_f %>% 
  mutate(cod = "all_cause") 

#checking if dates are the same: 
identical(
  sort(unique(covid$month_year)),
  sort(unique(all_repro_f$month_year))
)
## [1] TRUE
#finding dates missing 
setdiff(covid$month_year, all_repro_f$month_year)
## Date of length 0
setdiff(all_repro_f$month_year, covid$month_year)
## Date of length 0
combined <- all_repro_f %>%
  left_join(covid, 
            by = c("month_year"),
            suffix = c("_all", "_covid"))

#subtracting deaths, making NA deaths 0
all_cause_minus_covid <- combined %>%
  mutate(
    Deaths = Deaths_all - coalesce(Deaths_covid, 0)
  ) %>%
  select(month_year, Deaths)

head(all_cause_minus_covid)
##   month_year Deaths
## 1 2018-01-01    436
## 2 2018-02-01    378
## 3 2018-03-01    354
## 4 2018-04-01    336
## 5 2018-05-01    380
## 6 2018-06-01    355
#checking that we have every month! 
full_dates <- seq.Date(
  from = as.Date("2018-01-01"),
  to   = as.Date("2023-12-01"),
  by   = "month"
)

missing_dates <- setdiff(full_dates, all_cause_minus_covid$month_year)

missing_dates
## Date of length 0

After creating our all cause minus covid, it looks as though we have successfully subtracted all and preserved all the dates in our study period. We can now save this cleaned dataset.

write_csv(all_cause_minus_covid, "../CleanData/all_cause_minus_covid.csv")

9.1 Racial Disag

h_covid <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/COVID/H.csv")
nh_covid <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/COVID/NH.csv") 
head(h_covid)
##   Notes      Month Month.Code Deaths     Population     Crude.Rate
## 1       Jan., 2018    2018/01      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 2       Feb., 2018    2018/02      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 3       Mar., 2018    2018/03      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 4       Apr., 2018    2018/04      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 5        May, 2018    2018/05      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 6       Jun., 2018    2018/06      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
##   Crude.Rate.Lower.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                           Not Applicable
## 2                           Not Applicable
## 3                           Not Applicable
## 4                           Not Applicable
## 5                           Not Applicable
## 6                           Not Applicable
##   Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                           Not Applicable
## 2                           Not Applicable
## 3                           Not Applicable
## 4                           Not Applicable
## 5                           Not Applicable
## 6                           Not Applicable
head(nh_covid)
##   Notes      Month Month.Code                             Single.Race.6
## 1       Jan., 2018    2018/01          American Indian or Alaska Native
## 2       Jan., 2018    2018/01                                     Asian
## 3       Jan., 2018    2018/01                 Black or African American
## 4       Jan., 2018    2018/01 Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
## 5       Jan., 2018    2018/01                                     White
## 6       Jan., 2018    2018/01                        More than one race
##   Single.Race.6.Code Deaths     Population     Crude.Rate
## 1             1002-5      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 2                  A      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 3             2054-5      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 4              NHOPI      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 5             2106-3      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
## 6                  M      0 Not Applicable Not Applicable
##   Crude.Rate.Lower.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                           Not Applicable
## 2                           Not Applicable
## 3                           Not Applicable
## 4                           Not Applicable
## 5                           Not Applicable
## 6                           Not Applicable
##   Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                           Not Applicable
## 2                           Not Applicable
## 3                           Not Applicable
## 4                           Not Applicable
## 5                           Not Applicable
## 6                           Not Applicable
racial_covid <- clean_racial(nh_covid,h_covid)
head(racial_covid)
##                           race_eth Deaths month_year
## 1 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-01-01
## 2 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-02-01
## 3 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-03-01
## 4 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-04-01
## 5 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-05-01
## 6 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-06-01
racial_covid <- racial_covid %>% 
  mutate(cod = "covid") %>% 
  filter(!race_eth %in% c("Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander", "American Indian or Alaska Native"))  

h <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/All_cause/H.csv")
nh <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/All_cause/NH.csv") 
racial <- clean_racial(nh,h)
head(racial)
##                           race_eth Deaths month_year
## 1 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-01-01
## 2 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-07-01
## 3 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-08-01
## 4 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-10-01
## 5 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2018-11-01
## 6 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA 2019-01-01
#cleaning the two datasets: 
racial <- racial %>% 
  filter(!race_eth %in% c("Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander", "American Indian or Alaska Native")) %>% 
  mutate(cod = "all_cause")  

head(racial_covid)
##   race_eth Deaths month_year   cod
## 1    Asian     NA 2018-01-01 covid
## 2    Asian     NA 2018-02-01 covid
## 3    Asian     NA 2018-03-01 covid
## 4    Asian     NA 2018-04-01 covid
## 5    Asian     NA 2018-05-01 covid
## 6    Asian     NA 2018-06-01 covid
head(racial)
##   race_eth Deaths month_year       cod
## 1    Asian     14 2018-02-01 all_cause
## 2    Asian     10 2018-07-01 all_cause
## 3    Asian     12 2018-09-01 all_cause
## 4    Asian     10 2018-11-01 all_cause
## 5    Asian     12 2018-12-01 all_cause
## 6    Asian     12 2019-01-01 all_cause
#checking if dates are the same: 
identical(
  sort(unique(racial_covid$month_year)),
  sort(unique(racial$month_year))
)
## [1] TRUE
#combining
combined_racial <- racial %>%
  left_join(racial_covid, by = c("month_year", "race_eth"),
            suffix = c("_all", "_covid"))

#subtracting deaths, making NA deaths 0
racial_minus_covid <- combined_racial %>%
  mutate(
    Deaths = Deaths_all - coalesce(Deaths_covid, 0)
  ) %>%
  select(race_eth, month_year, Deaths)

head(racial_minus_covid)
##   race_eth month_year Deaths
## 1    Asian 2018-02-01     14
## 2    Asian 2018-07-01     10
## 3    Asian 2018-09-01     12
## 4    Asian 2018-11-01     10
## 5    Asian 2018-12-01     12
## 6    Asian 2019-01-01     12
#checking that we have every month! 
full_dates <- seq.Date(
  from = as.Date("2018-01-01"),
  to   = as.Date("2023-12-01"),
  by   = "month"
)

missing_dates <- setdiff(full_dates, racial_minus_covid$month_year)

missing_dates  
## Date of length 0
write_csv(racial_minus_covid, "../CleanData/racial_minus_covid.csv")

9.1.1 Annual

We are going to look at annual trends in terms of assault deaths for repro-age females, just in case there’s something to be said there.

h_assault <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/Assault/Annual/H.csv")
nh_assault <- read.csv("../RawData/RacialDisaggregation/Assault/Annual/NH.csv") 
head(h_assault)
##   Notes Year Year.Code Deaths Population Crude.Rate
## 1       2018      2018     89    2550647        3.5
## 2       2019      2019     76    2584352        2.9
## 3       2020      2020     98    2624855        3.7
## 4       2021      2021    100    2654764        3.8
## 5       2022      2022    107    2718383        3.9
## 6       2023      2023    117    2733117        4.3
##   Crude.Rate.Lower.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                                      2.8
## 2                                      2.3
## 3                                      3.0
## 4                                      3.0
## 5                                      3.2
## 6                                      3.5
##   Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                                      4.3
## 2                                      3.7
## 3                                      4.5
## 4                                      4.5
## 5                                      4.7
## 6                                      5.1
head(nh_assault)
##   Notes Year Year.Code                             Single.Race.6
## 1       2018      2018          American Indian or Alaska Native
## 2       2018      2018                                     Asian
## 3       2018      2018                 Black or African American
## 4       2018      2018 Native Hawaiian or Other Pacific Islander
## 5       2018      2018                                     White
## 6       2019      2019          American Indian or Alaska Native
##   Single.Race.6.Code     Deaths Population Crude.Rate
## 1             1002-5          0      18913        0.0
## 2                  A Suppressed     349407 Suppressed
## 3             2054-5         73     786516        9.3
## 4              NHOPI          0       6134        0.0
## 5             2106-3         53    2173079        2.4
## 6             1002-5          0      19128        0.0
##   Crude.Rate.Lower.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                                      0.0
## 2                               Suppressed
## 3                                      7.3
## 4                                      0.0
## 5                                      1.8
## 6                                      0.0
##   Crude.Rate.Upper.95..Confidence.Interval
## 1                                      0.0
## 2                               Suppressed
## 3                                     11.7
## 4                                      0.0
## 5                                      3.2
## 6                                      0.0
unique(nh_assault$Year)
##  [1] "2018"                           "2019"                          
##  [3] "2020"                           "2021"                          
##  [5] "2022"                           "2023"                          
##  [7] "2024"                           "2025 (provisional)"            
##  [9] "2026 (provisional and partial)" ""
unique(h_assault$Year)
##  [1] "2018"                           "2019"                          
##  [3] "2020"                           "2021"                          
##  [5] "2022"                           "2023"                          
##  [7] "2024"                           "2025 (provisional)"            
##  [9] "2026 (provisional and partial)" ""
racial_assault <- clean_racial_annual(nh_assault,h_assault)  


head(racial_assault) 
##   year                         race_eth deaths
## 1 2018 American Indian or Alaska Native      0
## 2 2019 American Indian or Alaska Native      0
## 3 2020 American Indian or Alaska Native      0
## 4 2021 American Indian or Alaska Native      0
## 5 2022 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA
## 6 2023 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA
racial_assault %>% 
  group_by(race_eth) %>% 
  filter(is.na(deaths)) 
## # A tibble: 9 × 3
## # Groups:   race_eth [2]
##    year race_eth                         deaths
##   <dbl> <chr>                             <dbl>
## 1  2022 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA
## 2  2023 American Indian or Alaska Native     NA
## 3  2018 Asian                                NA
## 4  2019 Asian                                NA
## 5  2020 Asian                                NA
## 6  2021 Asian                                NA
## 7  2022 Asian                                NA
## 8  2023 Asian                                NA
## 9  2024 Asian                                NA
#missing too much info for Asian and American Indian/Alaska Native, excluding those from df 

racial_assault <- racial_assault %>% 
  filter(!race_eth %in% c("American Indian or Alaska Native", "Asian")) 

write.csv(racial_assault, "../CleanData/racial_assault_annual.csv")
